name: Streamlit
what_it_does: Python-native framework for building interactive web apps and data dashboards with pure Python — no HTML, CSS, or JavaScript required. Write st.write(), st.chart(), st.slider() and Streamlit renders a live-reloading web app. Popular for LLM demos, ML model showcases, data exploration tools, and agent UIs. Acquired by Snowflake.
use_cases: Build LLM chat interfaces and agent UIs rapidly with st.chat_message() and st.chat_input() without frontend code
Create interactive agent demos with parameter sliders, file uploaders, and real-time output for stakeholder review
Prototype agent evaluation dashboards that display metrics, charts, and sample outputs from LLM runs
Build internal data exploration tools for agent team workflows with minimal development overhead
Deploy agent monitoring dashboards with st.metric(), charts, and auto-refresh for production visibility
not_for: Production multi-user web applications — Streamlit re-runs the entire script on every interaction; not designed for high-concurrency production apps
Complex UI with custom layouts, animations, or rich interactivity — use React, Vue, or Gradio for richer UI needs
Applications requiring user authentication — Streamlit Community Cloud has no auth; self-hosted requires st.secrets or third-party auth
best_when: You need to build an interactive Python data app, LLM demo, or agent UI in hours — especially for internal tools or prototypes where frontend development would take days.
avoid_when: You're building a production web app for external users with complex UI, auth, or high concurrency — use FastAPI + React or Gradio for more scalable setups.

auth.notes: No built-in auth. Streamlit Community Cloud has basic email sharing. Self-hosted requires reverse proxy auth or st_authenticator community library.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Open source library free. Streamlit Community Cloud: free tier with public/private app hosting limits
pricing.paid_tiers: Streamlit in Snowflake (enterprise integration); Community Cloud workspace upgrades
pricing.notes: Apache 2.0 license. Community Cloud hosting free for limited apps. Snowflake integration available for enterprise.

performance.rate_limits: Community Cloud: app limits per account. Self-hosted: limited by server resources. Single-threaded per session.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Streamlit displays Python exceptions in the browser with tracebacks. DeltaGenerator errors include widget key conflicts. Clear error messages for most common mistakes.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Streamlit re-runs the full script on every interaction — agent code inside Streamlit must be idempotent or use st.cache_resource/st.cache_data to prevent repeated initialization.
known_agent_gotchas: Streamlit re-runs the entire Python script on every user interaction — expensive initializations (model loading, DB connections) must be wrapped in @st.cache_resource to avoid re-running
Session state must use st.session_state for persistence across reruns — regular Python variables reset on every script execution
Streaming LLM output requires st.write_stream() or manual placeholder updating with st.empty() — standard streaming patterns need adaptation for Streamlit's execution model
Widget keys must be unique across the app — duplicate keys cause StremLitAPIException; use dynamic keys with f-strings for widgets in loops
Streamlit runs in a single thread per user session — CPU-intensive operations block the UI; use st.spinner() and run heavy work in threads or subprocesses
Community Cloud deployments require secrets in .streamlit/secrets.toml or the dashboard — don't hardcode API keys in app.py which is public on GitHub

security_score_components.security_notes: No built-in auth in open source. st.secrets provides secret management for Community Cloud. SOC2 compliance for Streamlit Cloud hosting. Snowflake integration adds enterprise governance.
